mission: Provision the canonical event spine, queueing substrate, and durable outbox and inbox infrastructure for Vecells so canonical events map to concrete broker topology, explicit retry and DLQ posture, and replay-safe command and callback checkpoints.

assumptions.reason: The mock-now path needs durable streams, queue groups, and replay-safe consumers without requiring a live managed broker during local or CI runs.
Canonical event names remain canonical; the broker may group them by namespace stream but may not invent transport aliases.

follow_on_dependencies.scope: Cache invalidation and live-update fan-out overlays consume the same event spine later.
Distributed tracing and correlation telemetry may extend these checkpoints but not change queue law.
Migration and projection backfill runners consume the published replay queue and checkpoint manifests.

queueGroups.notes: Live projection consumers replay from immutable canonical events only.
Cold rebuild and dry-run replay stay separate from live projection apply.
External effects publish only after durable outbox checkpoint and effect-key reservation.
Delivery posture preserves receipt ambiguity and callback repair without silent resend loops.
Provider callbacks bind to explicit correlation keys and ordered receipt windows.
Assurance slices observe the same canonical events without shadow audit traffic.
Identity-preserving quarantine routes hold canonical events for governed replay review.
